title: Sex Ed

jokes.text: We don't go with that man. I've seen several men go with that man and none come back. We've lost friends... We don't know what he does with them.
jokes.setup: Day laborers discussing someone who picks up workers
jokes.punchline: Ominous implication that workers disappear forever
jokes.explanation: Dark setup for Dwight's elaborate prank. Creates genuine tension before the reveal.

dialogue.speaker: Day Laborer 1
dialogue.line: Hey, you see that guy who picks up workers from here every morning?

dialogue.speaker: Day Laborer 2
dialogue.line: Yeah, why?

dialogue.speaker: Day Laborer 1
dialogue.line: Nobody's ever seen them again.

dialogue.speaker: Day Laborer 2
dialogue.line: What do you mean 'again'?

dialogue.speaker: Day Laborer 1
dialogue.line: I mean... they never come back.

jokes.text: I pick up day laborers and tell them that they'll get paid at 6:00 p.m. At 5:45, a certain I.N.S. Agent, by the name of Mose Schrute, throws them in the back of a van, drops them off in the middle of Harrisburg and tells them it's Canada.
jokes.setup: Mysterious disappearance of day laborers
jokes.punchline: Elaborate fake deportation scheme involving telling them Harrisburg is Canada
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight absurdity - only he would orchestrate fake deportations. The specificity of 'Harrisburg as Canada' is brilliant.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I have a solution to this labor shortage. We tell them Harrisburg is Canada.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: We round up all the day laborers, we put them on a bus, we drive them to Harrisburg, and we tell them it's Canada. Problem solved.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Dwight, that's insane.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Not insane. Efficient.

jokes.text: Scranton. Y... before that, la Philadelphia.
jokes.setup: Dwight asking where worker is from in broken Spanish
jokes.punchline: Worker is completely local - from Scranton and Philadelphia
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic payoff - Dwight's elaborate immigrant worker scheme targets locals.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: ¿De dónde eres? Where are you from?

dialogue.speaker: Day Laborer
dialogue.line: Scranton. I'm from Scranton.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: No, no, before that. Where are you *really* from?

dialogue.speaker: Day Laborer
dialogue.line: Philadelphia.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Ah, sí, sí.

jokes.text: Yeah. I got stung up my dress. Poor hornet.
jokes.setup: Phyllis complaining about getting stung by hornets
jokes.punchline: Dwight expressing sympathy for the hornet instead of Phyllis
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight callousness delivered with perfect economy. Only two words but perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: I got stung by hornets!

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Those poor hornets. They were just defending their nest.

jokes.text: If he chooses correctly, he'll conquer the hornets. And if he doesn't? He'll die. What? Beg your pardon?
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining the hornet situation as 'do or die'
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation that Nate will actually die if he fails
jokes.explanation: Dwight's literal mindset creates dark humor. Pam's confused reaction sells it.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: This is a do or die situation.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: So Nate will actually die if he fails?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Yes.

jokes.text: When did the phrase 'do or die' become so corrupted?
jokes.setup: Dwight literally interpreting 'do or die'
jokes.punchline: Dwight thinking everyone else corrupted the phrase, not him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight logic - everyone else is wrong about common phrases.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: The phrase is 'do or die.' It means you must succeed or face death. I have prepared accordingly.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Dwight, that's not what it means.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Oh, so now you're all suddenly experts on the English language? I know what 'or' means. You people have corrupted this phrase with your participation trophy nonsense.

jokes.text: Visual: Nate attempting to deal with hornets using increasingly inappropriate tools while everyone watches in horror
jokes.setup: Nate needs to remove hornet's nest
jokes.punchline: Series of terrible tool choices culminating in him getting stung
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy escalation. Functional but not particularly creative.

dialogue.speaker: Nate
dialogue.line: I'll remove that hornet's nest.

dialogue.speaker: Office Worker
dialogue.line: How are you going to do that?

dialogue.speaker: Nate
dialogue.line: I'll use this spatula.

dialogue.speaker: Office Worker
dialogue.line: A spatula?

dialogue.speaker: Nate
dialogue.line: If that doesn't work, I have a hairdryer.

dialogue.speaker: Office Worker
dialogue.line: A hairdryer?

dialogue.speaker: Nate
dialogue.line: And if that fails, I'll try this yo-yo.

dialogue.speaker: Nate
dialogue.line: Ow! Ow! Ow!

jokes.text: Good morning, Erin. Any mustaches, I mean messages?
jokes.setup: Michael wearing fake mustache
jokes.punchline: Freudian slip mixing 'mustaches' and 'messages'
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay joke that sets up Michael's disguise subplot. Adequate but unremarkable.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm not just any temp, I'm a temp with a mustache. It makes me look distinguished. People take me more seriously when I wear it.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I've been getting so many positive messages... I mean, mustaches. So many positive mustaches.

jokes.text: There are many reasons a man would wear a fake mustache to work. He is a fan of the outrageous. He loves to surprise! He loves other things as well.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining legitimate reasons for fake mustache
jokes.punchline: Vague implication of sexual reasons with 'other things'
jokes.explanation: Michael's inability to fully commit to his lie creates awkward humor.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: There are legitimate reasons to wear a fake mustache. Disguises, theatrical productions, and... other things.

jokes.text: It's a pimple, Phyllis. Avril Lavigne gets them all the time, and she rocks harder than anyone alive.
jokes.setup: Michael defending having a pimple
jokes.punchline: Random Avril Lavigne reference as authority on rock credibility
jokes.explanation: Michael's bizarre logic and pop culture references. The randomness is very him.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: It's not a pimple, it's a beauty mark. Avril Lavigne has one.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: She's very credible when it comes to rock and roll.

jokes.text: You mean cancer? What? No way. No. Definitely not. Not cancer.
jokes.setup: Phyllis suggesting it's not a pimple
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately jumping to cancer as next possibility
jokes.explanation: Michael's hypochondriac tendencies and tendency to catastrophize.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: I don't think that's a pimple.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oh my God, is it cancer?

jokes.text: It's just good to catch a Michael train of thought early before it derails and destroys the entire town.
jokes.setup: Michael spiraling about having cancer
jokes.punchline: Jim's metaphor about Michael's thought process as destructive train
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor that perfectly captures Michael's mental spirals. Great Jim observation.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: It's like watching a train that has no brakes, no conductor, and is heading directly toward a station full of people who are all screaming at it to stop, but the train just keeps going faster because it thinks it's helping.

jokes.text: A cold sore is herpes. What?
jokes.setup: Michael relieved it's just a cold sore
jokes.punchline: Meredith's blunt revelation that cold sores are herpes
jokes.explanation: Meredith as the bearer of uncomfortable truths. Michael's shock is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oh thank God, it's just a cold sore.

dialogue.speaker: Meredith
dialogue.line: That's herpes.

jokes.text: I know tons and tons of people who have herpes. I have it myself. That's what it is.
jokes.setup: Discussion of herpes stigma
jokes.punchline: Meredith casually revealing she has herpes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Meredith overshare. Her casual attitude makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Meredith
dialogue.line: You know, people make such a big deal about herpes. I've had it for years and it's really not that bad.

jokes.text: I've never seen herpes on you. 'Cause it's on my genitals, genius.
jokes.setup: Michael's naive observation about not seeing Meredith's herpes
jokes.punchline: Meredith's blunt explanation of genital herpes location
jokes.explanation: Michael's innocence meets Meredith's vulgarity. Her 'genius' is perfectly dismissive.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I don't understand, I've never seen your herpes.

dialogue.speaker: Meredith
dialogue.line: That's because it's on my genitals, Michael.

jokes.text: You have a penis?
jokes.setup: Meredith talking about genital herpes
jokes.punchline: Michael's confused question about Meredith having a penis
jokes.explanation: Michael's confusion about female anatomy creates absurd moment.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Wait, you have a penis?

jokes.text: Michael, you're at least 46. Why 'at least'? If you're guessing 46, just say 46.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Michael's age for medical history
jokes.punchline: Michael's defensive reaction to 'at least' implying he could be older
jokes.explanation: Michael's vanity about age comes through in his defensive parsing of Oscar's words.

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: How old are you?

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: At least.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: What do you mean 'at least'? That implies I could be older!

jokes.text: This is what you get when you treat your body like an outhouse.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Meredith's sexual health
jokes.punchline: Angela's prissy condemnation using outhouse metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela prudishness with a vivid, judgmental metaphor.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: I don't even want to know what goes on in Meredith's personal life. It's like looking into an outhouse.

jokes.text: The number one sexually transmitted disease is ignorance.
jokes.setup: Andy taking charge of sex education discussion
jokes.punchline: Pretentious PSA-style proclamation about ignorance as STD
jokes.explanation: Andy's try-hard wisdom and RA background create character-perfect moment.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Ignorance is the STD of the mind.

jokes.text: I feel like one of those old-timey sailors with VD. (IN GRUFF VOICE) Eh, it's me own darn fault. Girl in every port!
jokes.setup: Michael processing having an STD
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous sailor voice and persona despite his limited dating history
jokes.explanation: Michael's theatrical self-pity with a character voice. The irony of his limited experience makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm not just sick, I'm a man of the sea now. Arrr, me hearty! *adjusts imaginary captain's hat* Who knew my limited dating history could lead to such exotic ports of call?

jokes.text: What port? The Jan port? The Holly port?
jokes.setup: Michael's 'girl in every port' claim
jokes.punchline: Jim naming Michael's very limited romantic history as 'ports'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim deflation of Michael's grandiose self-image with specific callback humor.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: So these 'ports' would be... what, exactly? Because I'm pretty sure the only port Michael's been to is the one time he went to Sandals Jamaica and immediately got sunburned.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's not true, I've been to many—

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: And then there's Jan. And... I think that's actually it. So Michael's got a girl in every port, and he's been to one port.

jokes.text: Now, I'm no doctor, but it seems to me that we all have an obligation to the public health to track down anyone who gives us a disease, inform them of it, and take overwhelming revenge on that person. Again, I'm no doctor. I'm just a normal guy who enjoys revenge.
jokes.setup: Dwight offering medical advice about contacting partners
jokes.punchline: Escalating from public health to 'overwhelming revenge' while claiming normalcy
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight - reasonable start that spirals into his revenge obsession. 'I'm just a normal guy who enjoys revenge' is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: If you've had sexual contact with someone, it is your civic duty to inform all of your previous partners immediately. It's basic public health.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I've already started a list. I'm working through it alphabetically. When I'm done, I'm going to do a second pass for overwhelming revenge.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: But that's just me being thorough. It's nothing personal.

jokes.text: I have a disease for which there is no known cure that has been sexually transmitted to me. Oh, no! I can't even say it. H-I-R-P-E-E-S.
jokes.setup: Michael dramatically revealing his diagnosis to Donna
jokes.punchline: Spelling herpes wrong while trying to be dramatic
jokes.explanation: Michael's dramatic flair undermined by basic spelling error. Classic Michael incompetence.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Donna, I have something to tell you. Something serious. I have... herpees.

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: You mean herpes?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's what I said. Herpees.

jokes.text: What do you mean 'again'? You're always asking for our attention. Well, maybe like a year ago. Mmm. Seems recent.
jokes.setup: Andy asking for attention for announcement
jokes.punchline: Stanley's matter-of-fact observation about Andy's attention-seeking pattern
jokes.explanation: Stanley's dry observation deflates Andy's moment. Good character dynamics.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Everyone, I have an announcement to make.

dialogue.speaker: Stanley
dialogue.line: You always do.

jokes.text: Oh! When you got your new phone, that's when you asked for everyone's attention. World Cup. That's what I was thinking. You kept announcing scores.
jokes.setup: Andy defending his attention-asking frequency
jokes.punchline: Multiple specific examples piling up, proving the point
jokes.explanation: Good escalation as more examples pile up, proving Andy wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I don't ask for attention that much.

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: Andy, you asked me what I thought about your haircut three times in one day.

dialogue.speaker: Stanley
dialogue.line: You made us all listen to your a cappella group perform for forty minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: That was a one-time thing.

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: You've done it twice since then.

dialogue.speaker: Stanley
dialogue.line: And you asked everyone in this office to rate your performance on a scale of one to ten.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I was just trying to improve.

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: You asked me to rate you while I was in the bathroom.

jokes.text: This is Holly. No, this is Holly. No... This is Michael Scott. Busted.
jokes.setup: Michael calling Holly and pretending to be someone else
jokes.punchline: Immediately failing at deception and Holly calling him out
jokes.explanation: Michael's incompetence at deception. Holly's 'Busted' is perfectly timed.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Hello, this is... uh... Bob Vance, Vance Refrigeration.

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: Michael, that's you.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No it's not.

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: Yes it is. I can hear you.

jokes.text: Were they in the missionary position?
jokes.setup: Holly mentioning missionaries in fake car accident
jokes.punchline: Michael's juvenile sexual pun on 'missionary'
jokes.explanation: Predictable Michael juvenile humor. Low-hanging fruit but in character.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Missionary? That's the position I use!

jokes.text: We would have twins. I don't think we'd have kids. Mmm-hmm. We would be married. Michael, I've been dating A.J. For a year and a half now.
jokes.setup: Michael fantasizing about alternate reality with Holly
jokes.punchline: Holly gently but firmly shutting down his fantasy with reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe as Michael's delusion meets Holly's gentle reality check.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: In my fantasy, we're married with three kids, a house in the suburbs, and you quit your job to be with me.

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: Michael, that's not going to happen. I have my own career, and we work together. This isn't healthy.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: But Holly—

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: No. We need to focus on work. That's it.

jokes.text: You cried at that tag line for a movie you made up. 'He had no arms or legs. He couldn't see, hear or speak. This is how he led a nation.'
jokes.setup: Holly explaining Michael's tendency to romanticize
jokes.punchline: Specific callback to Michael's absurd movie pitch
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that perfectly captures Michael's absurd sentimentality. The escalating disability list is inspired.

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: Michael has this thing where he romanticizes everything. Like, he once pitched a movie idea called 'Threat Level Midnight' where he was a secret agent who could seduce women with his wits and also solve crimes.

jokes.text: How do I do it? Raise my daughter, work as director of office purchasing for this hospital and release an album of Doris Day covers on my own label? If I knew, I'd tell you.
jokes.setup: Jan appearing successful and put-together
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of bizarre Doris Day cover album project
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jan absurdity - the Doris Day album comes out of nowhere and lands perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Jan
dialogue.line: I've been really focused on my career lately. Things are going great at the office, and I'm feeling really put-together, you know?

dialogue.speaker: Jan
dialogue.line: Actually, I'm working on this passion project right now. I'm producing a Doris Day cover album.

jokes.text: (SINGING) Fry it up in a pan, Never, never, never let you forget you're a man. Love that commercial. I don't understand the reference.
jokes.setup: Jan singing old commercial jingle enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: Michael's blank response showing generational/cultural gap
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Jan's corporate background vs Michael's obliviousness.

dialogue.speaker: Jan
dialogue.line: ♪ Folgers in my cup ♪

jokes.text: Now I'm gonna show you another picture of perfectly normal genitalia. (ALL EXCLAIM IN DISGUST) Oh, my God! Yeah, sure, it's got some herpes on it. But you know what? It's just as normal as anyone else's.
jokes.setup: Andy's sex education presentation with genital photos
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning the herpes after everyone's already disgusted
jokes.explanation: Andy's misguided sensitivity training backfires spectacularly with poor timing.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: And here we see the herpes simplex virus.

dialogue.speaker: Office Workers
dialogue.line: *groans and recoils*

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: What? I thought that would be the easy part.

jokes.text: Well, if there was anything exciting about it, it was because we both knew it was wrong. Because we worked together. No. Okay, imagine there's a princess who falls for a guy beneath her station, and the queen doesn't like this at all... Am I the princess? No, I'm the princess. And the queen.
jokes.setup: Jan explaining their relationship dynamic with princess metaphor
jokes.punchline: Jan casting herself as both princess AND disapproving queen
jokes.explanation: Jan's narcissistic psychology perfectly captured in convoluted metaphor where she's multiple characters.

dialogue.speaker: Jan
dialogue.line: I'm like a princess, and you're like my knight in shining armor.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's sweet.

dialogue.speaker: Jan
dialogue.line: But I'm also the queen, and I'm very disappointed in you.

jokes.text: (SINGING) How was school? It was cool. What did you learn? What did I learn? You might have learned shapes, or blocks, or clocks, or colors, or you might have learned that we're all sisters and brothers... I have herpes.
jokes.setup: Jan singing sweet educational song with her daughter
jokes.punchline: Abrupt transition to herpes revelation mid-song
jokes.explanation: Brilliant tonal whiplash from saccharine children's song to medical revelation. Perfect Jan moment.

dialogue.speaker: Jan
dialogue.line: ♪ The itsy bitsy spider climbed up the water spout ♪

dialogue.speaker: Jan
dialogue.line: ♪ Down came the rain and washed the spider out ♪

dialogue.speaker: Jan
dialogue.line: ♪ Out came the sun and dried up all the rain ♪

dialogue.speaker: Jan
dialogue.line: ♪ And the itsy bitsy spider climbed up the spout again... ♪

dialogue.speaker: Jan
dialogue.line: Also, I have herpes.

jokes.text: Just admit that your baby was a mistake. Whoa! Hey, our baby was not a mistake. She was a surprise.
jokes.setup: Andy pushing for honesty about unplanned pregnancy consequences
jokes.punchline: Jim and Pam's defensive distinction between 'mistake' and 'surprise'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jim/Pam's sensitivity about their timing while Andy blunders on.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Look, I'm just saying, you two need to be honest about the fact that having a baby wasn't planned.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: It wasn't a mistake.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: It was a surprise.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: What's the difference?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: A surprise is something you didn't expect but you're happy about.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: A mistake is something you regret.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: We don't regret Cece.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: We regret a lot of things, but not Cece.

jokes.text: You know, I don't know if I trust Jan's judgment. She... Jan knows paper. No, not paper. Relationships.
jokes.setup: Michael questioning Jan's judgment
jokes.punchline: Dwight's literal-minded correction about Jan's expertise
jokes.explanation: Dwight's literal mindset clashing with Michael's emotional processing.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Jan's judgment? Come on, she's a VP at a major paper company. She knows what she's doing.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Actually, Jan is VP of Sales. She's not qualified to make judgments about paper quality or production. That's not her expertise.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Dwight, that's not the point.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: It's exactly the point. You can't just assume someone knows something because of their title.

jokes.text: Who are you? I'm Michael. We dated for a while. I don't think so. Michael? Oh, hey! Dwight, would you take my grandmother for a walk while I talk to the real Helene?
jokes.setup: Michael approaching the wrong woman
jokes.punchline: Awkward realization he's hitting on grandmother instead of Helene
jokes.explanation: Standard mistaken identity gag. Functional but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Hey there, beautiful. Can I buy you a drink?

dialogue.speaker: Helene's Grandmother
dialogue.line: Oh my! Well, aren't you charming.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: So what's a lovely woman like you doing here alone?

dialogue.speaker: Helene
dialogue.line: Dad, that's my grandmother.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: What? Oh God. Oh no. I... I thought... That's not... I'm so sorry.

jokes.text: What's the safest way to go skiing? Don't ski. (ALL LAUGHING)
jokes.setup: Andy's trick question about safe sex vs abstinence
jokes.punchline: Darryl's parallel logic about skiing safety
jokes.explanation: Perfect parallel logic that deflates Andy's sanctimonious point with humor and brevity.

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: You know what? That's like asking me if I'd rather wear a helmet when I ski or just not ski. I'm gonna ski, man. I love skiing.

jokes.text: Why would you choose a pencil, Andy? Well, I'm not gonna use my penis, Oscar. It's not exactly hard right now, anyway.
jokes.setup: Oscar questioning Andy's condom demonstration prop choice
jokes.punchline: Andy's overshare about his current arousal state
jokes.explanation: Andy's tendency to overshare under pressure creates cringe humor.

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: Why would you use that as a condom demonstration prop?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Well, I was aroused when I bought it.

jokes.text: Come on, give it a rest, pencil (BLEEP). I'm doing this for you, Meredith!
jokes.setup: Andy's awkward condom demonstration
jokes.punchline: Stanley's dismissive insult calling Andy a pencil dick
jokes.explanation: Stanley's blunt hostility toward Andy's presentation. The pencil connection is clever.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: So this is how you properly apply a condom...

dialogue.speaker: Stanley
dialogue.line: You're a pencil dick.

jokes.text: I think that for you to have come here even expecting that we could have a conversation like this shows how self-deluded you are. Michael, your memory has failed you greatly.
jokes.setup: Michael's romantic nostalgia about their relationship
jokes.punchline: Helene's brutal honesty about Michael's delusions
jokes.explanation: Harsh reality check for Michael's tendency to rewrite history. Well-earned cringe.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I was just thinking about us, you know? All those good times we had together.

dialogue.speaker: Helene
dialogue.line: Michael, we dated for like three weeks. And you spent most of that time talking about your ex-girlfriend.

jokes.text: You didn't call my office and ask them where I was? No! (LAUGHS NERVOUSLY) That's so... 'Cause the receptionist told me that a man called, but he would only say that he was my ex-Iover.
jokes.setup: Michael pretending to randomly encounter Carole
jokes.punchline: Carole catching him in obvious lie about stalking her
jokes.explanation: Michael caught red-handed in his deception. His nervous laugh sells the cringe.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oh, Carole! What a coincidence running into you here!

dialogue.speaker: Carole
dialogue.line: Michael, you're parked outside my house.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I know, I'm... I'm doing a stakeout. For work.

dialogue.speaker: Carole
dialogue.line: Your car is running and you're holding binoculars.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Birdwatching. Very dedicated to ornithology.

jokes.text: My good sir, nothing would make me happier than to hand you the hand of the hand once in my hand.
jokes.setup: Andy recalling giving Gabe permission to date Erin
jokes.punchline: Convoluted, overly formal phrasing that confuses everyone
jokes.explanation: Peak Andy pretentiousness with unnecessarily complex wordplay that undermines itself.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I said, 'You have my permission to court Ms. Hannon, contingent upon the understanding that said courtship shall be conducted with the utmost propriety and in accordance with the established protocols of this office, as well as the state of Pennsylvania.'

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Yeah, what does that mean?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: It means he can date her.

jokes.text: You proposed to me on our fourth date. Well, I believe in love at first sight. Well, so do I, but we didn't love each other at first, either.
jokes.setup: Michael defending his romantic impulsiveness
jokes.punchline: Carole's matter-of-fact dismissal that they never loved each other
jokes.explanation: Devastating reality check delivered with casual brutality. Perfect cringe moment.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm a romantic! I do things on impulse. That's who I am.

dialogue.speaker: Carol
dialogue.line: We never loved each other.

jokes.text: Someone died in the upstairs bathroom, didn't they? No.
jokes.setup: Dwight touring house with Carole
jokes.punchline: Random morbid question about bathroom deaths
jokes.explanation: Pure Dwight non-sequitur. His morbid curiosity perfectly timed and character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: So this bathroom, how many people have died in here?

dialogue.speaker: Carole
dialogue.line: What? No one has died in this bathroom.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That you know of.

jokes.text: Are you crying? No, I'm just sweating. I don't know what's got you upset, but my advice is stop crying. I'm not crying. I'm sweating.
jokes.setup: Andy emotional about Erin situation
jokes.punchline: Andy's denial about crying vs sweating, repeated
jokes.explanation: Simple running gag about Andy's transparent denial. Adequate but not inspired.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I'm not crying, I'm sweating.

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: Your eyes are leaking.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: It's sweat. From my face.

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: Sweat doesn't come out of your eyes.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Well, mine does.

jokes.text: I have no idea what his problem is. It's just my standard advice. It's good advice, right?
jokes.setup: Darryl giving generic pep talk to obviously heartbroken Andy
jokes.punchline: Darryl's complete obliviousness to Andy's emotional state
jokes.explanation: Darryl's emotional detachment creates humor through contrast with Andy's crisis.

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: You know what you need? You need to get back out there. Meet some new people. Have some fun.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Darryl, I'm really not in the headspace for—

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: Nah, nah, nah. Trust me on this one. You're a catch. You're funny, you're got a good job, you're a solid guy. Some girl is gonna be lucky to have you.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: That's... that's nice of you to say, but—

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: So what do you say? You wanna hit up that bar tonight? I'll be your wingman.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Yeah... no, I think I just need some time alone right now.

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: That's the spirit! Positive attitude. That's what I'm talking about.

jokes.text: This is Holly Flax. I can't come to the phone right now, but please leave a message at the sound of the tiny truck backing up.
jokes.setup: Standard voicemail greeting
jokes.punchline: Quirky 'tiny truck backing up' instead of beep
jokes.explanation: Perfect Holly quirk - whimsical and specific in a way that shows her personality.

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: Hi, you've reached Holly Flax at Wernham Woodson. I'm unable to take your call right now, but please leave a message after the tiny truck backing up.
[tiny truck backing up sound]

jokes.text: Today, I ended up seeing a lot of the women that I used to date, and in my mind, they were all great. And then when I actually saw them, it was mostly a freak show.
jokes.setup: Michael reflecting on his day of contacting ex-girlfriends
jokes.punchline: Brutal honesty about how disappointing his exes were in reality
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of Michael self-awareness. 'Freak show' is perfectly harsh yet accurate.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I called all my exes today. You know what I realized? They're all pretty disappointing in person. Like, I had built them up in my head to be these amazing women, but then I actually talked to them and I was like, 'Oh. You're just... regular.'

jokes.text: I remember every second of us. And talking to you today, I don't feel for them anything like what I feel for you. It's... I didn't joke with any of them. I joked with you.
jokes.setup: Michael's voicemail to Holly
jokes.punchline: Genuine emotional revelation about their connection
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment where Michael finds the right words. The 'I joked with you' line is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Hey Holly, it's me. I just wanted to say that... I know we don't always see eye to eye on everything, but when I'm around you, I feel like I can be myself. And that's rare. That's really rare. So I just wanted you to know that you mean a lot to me. Okay, bye.

jokes.text: We once sucked face in public as part of an office presentation to destroy the stigma about gay kissing. Do you recall?
jokes.setup: Michael contacting Oscar about potential herpes transmission
jokes.punchline: Referencing their awkward public kiss from previous episode
jokes.explanation: Good callback that brings uncomfortable memory into new uncomfortable context.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oscar, we need to talk about what happened at the Christmas party.

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: Michael, that was just a kiss on the cheek. It didn't mean anything.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I know, but I think I might have herpes.

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: What? From a kiss on the cheek?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm just being cautious. You never know these days.

jokes.text: I'm gonna need a list of every man you've ever had sex with. I'm talking train stations, men's rooms... Flower shops, fireworks celebrations... Fence with a hole in it. Moonlit gondola. Carriage ride through Central Park.
jokes.setup: Michael asking Oscar for list of sexual partners
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of increasingly romantic and specific gay hookup locations
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from seedy to romantic locations. The specificity and Michael's earnestness make it work.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oscar, I need you to make me a list of all your sexual partners.

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: Why would I do that?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: For diversity purposes. Come on, it'll just take a minute.

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: Michael, no.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. I'll just start: the park, the river, that rest stop on I-90...

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: Those aren't even—

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Wait, wait. The botanical gardens at sunset, that beautiful bed and breakfast in Vermont, that really tasteful cabin rental in Sedona...

tmdb_overview: Michael, believing he has herpes, contacts all his ex-girlfriends, including Jan and Holly. Andy tries to play on Erin’s compassionate side by holding a sex education meeting for the office.
insight: Cringe discomfort drives an 81-score episode with Michael's sex ed disaster as anchor.
